other_observers: Observed with Christian Nunes (finder) et al.;
specific_location: Small pond along Boulder Creek just upstream from Foothills Parkway, on the CU East campus.
description: A small flycatcher with greenish tones on the upperparts. Song and call were primary ID confirmation for me, but size along with head and body proportions immediately suggested Empidonax genus. Green tones, weak (at best) eye-ring, largish bill suggest Trail's-type empid.Behaviors: Foraging in willows along small pond.Call: Both call and song heard. Song the typical, iconic two-part zree zreet, upslurred on end every time. Call heard as I got closer, a very flat, plain short peet. I compared the call with that on Sibley's eguide in the field in real time and found the match to be perfect.Plumage: Adult
